source_jobs.title: Working Student / Master Thesis Student – Chemical Operations (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
🌍 Shape the Future of Climate Tech with Phlair!

Fossil fuels have powered our economy for decades—at the cost of pollution and a changing climate. At Phlair, we’re reversing that equation. With our cost-efficient, scalable electrochemical Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology, we pull CO₂ from the air to either store it permanently or repurpose it into CO₂-negative chemicals. Join us in enabling a carbon-negative future.




🚀 WHY WE’RE DIFFERENT?

We’ve developed a breakthrough hydrolizer for acid/base regeneration, enabling scalable CO₂ capture. With partners like Frontier Climate, Stripe, and Shopify, we’re on track to make a global impact.

Our Project Dawn is our flagship initiative, setting the foundation to scale our technology and remove millions of tons of CO₂ from the atmosphere. We're one of the only seven DAC companies with a proven offtake agreement. Phlair is poised to become a technology leader, leveraging renewable energy and low-cost DAC to provide clean energy solutions that go beyond carbon removal.




🛠️ YOUR MISSION AT PHLAIR

 - Hands-on plant support. Executing the planned maintenance backlog, managing liquid levels, pulling process samples, and documenting operational parameters.

 - Executing mechanical adjustments and retrofits with hand tools. Assisting the Operations Engineer in troubleshooting anomalies.

 - Project management for unit upgrades. Reviewing and updating P&IDs, conducting hazard and safety evaluations, and communicating with suppliers to procure new components.

Your targets are your North Star. Nothing is set in stone in our world, we adapt fast, but we always hit our goals.




🥼 WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

 - Currently enrolled in a B.Sc. or M.Sc. program in Chemical Engineering, Process Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a closely related field.

 - Demonstrated mechanical aptitude (comfortable using tools and physically assembling/disassembling equipment).

 - Familiarity with fluid mechanics, basic P&ID drafting, understanding of standard hazard evaluation processes (e.g., HAZOP basics).

 - Basic understanding of chemical/process engineering fundamentals.

 - Ability to read and understand technical drawings (P&IDs).

 - High autonomy, proactive communication, high attention to detail, and a rigorous safety mindset.




🌟 ESSENTIAL TRAITS

 - Hands-on execution: You’re not afraid to get your hands dirty and physically work on the plant.

 - Safety-first mindset: You don’t compromise on operational safety and hazard evaluations.




💡GREAT TO HAVE, BUT OPTIONAL

 - Prior experience operating, building, or maintaining a laboratory setup or pilot plant.

 - Background knowledge or high passion for Direct Air Capture (DAC) or carbon removal technologies.

 - Previous experience dealing with suppliers and component procurement.




🏭 HOW WE WORK AT PHLAIR

 - In-person matters: We work fully onsite in Ismaning, Germany. Our pilot plant is here, our breakthroughs happen here, and our team wins here – together.
   
   Goals matter: We do everything to achieve our goals. If your workstream is the bottleneck, you don't stop. This isn't about serving hours, it's about hitting goals that matter.
   
   Leadership means showing up and doing the work. We get that experienced people have more commitments, but championship teams require championship effort.




🌱 WHAT WE OFFER?

 - Competitive salary

 - 20 vacation days, based on a 40h work week

 - Public transport pass for all of Germany

 - Fitness and wellness benefits to keep you at your best (EGYM Wellpass)

 - Fresh team lunches, prepared by our amazing chef, Amelie

 - Direct leadership access to our CEO, CTO, and CSO




🔗 LEARN MORE ABOUT PHLAIR

 - Our Mission, Vision and Team https://phlair.com/careers

 - Phlair Secures Frontier’s First Electrochemical DAC Deal https://frontierclimate.com/writing/phlair

 - Technology Behind Phlair https://techcrunch.com/2024/09/19/phlairs-carbon-sucking-technology-could-lower-direct-air-ca

source_jobs.title: Electrical Test Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
🌍 BUILD THE FUTURE OF CLIMATE TECH WITH PHLAIR

We're solving humanity's biggest challenge, and it takes everything we've got.

The world needs more energy, not less. Fossil fuels powered our economy for decades, at the cost of our planet. At Phlair, we're inverting that equation with breakthrough electrochemical Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology that pulls CO₂ from the air for permanent storage or repurposing into carbon-negative chemicals.

We're not here to play it safe. We're here to win.




WHY WE’RE DIFFERENT?

We solve hard problems and are product-first. Our hydrolyzer-based Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology, enabling scalable and low-cost CO2 capture from ambient air. The electrochemical technology allows off-the-shelf components and is ideally suited to the intermittent nature of renewable energy sources.

With proven offtake agreements from customers such as Google, McKinsey, JPMorganChase, Shopify, Stripe, H&M, and Deep Sky; we're one of only seven DAC companies with real market validation.

Project Dawn isn't just our flagship; it's our shot to scale CO₂ removal globally.




IS THIS A FIT FOR ME?

We're building for the 3-5% who are energized by winning and thrive under pressure. Our pace demands people who see excellence as non-negotiable and execution as everything. We cherish work-life-harmony, not balance. If you need extensive structure or prefer playing it safe, we're probably not the right fit.

Don't tick every box? We value potential and spikes over perfect resumes. If you believe you can contribute to our mission at this intensity level, tell us why.




YOUR MISSION

 - Test Plan Development: Collaborate with our CSO Steffen https://www.linkedin.com/in/steffen-garbe-017425143/ to design and execute tests for evaluating the functionality and compliance of our electrochemical stack for our 260 tCO2/year DAC demonstrator.

 - Collaborate: Work with the team to develop internal and external test setups.

 - Infrastructure Setup & Maintenance: Set up and maintain the test infrastructure, with a focus on quality assurance and reliability. Electrical wiring qualifications are a plus.

 - Data Analysis: Analyze test data and communicate key results to the development team.

 - Adapt & Innovate: In the fast-paced startup world, your ability to adapt to an evolving environment and quickly pivot will be crucial. You’ll work autonomously, learn on the go, and contribute to shaping a new sector.

Your targets are your North Star. Nothing is set in stone in our world, we adapt fast, but we always hit our goals.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Education: M.Sc. in Electrical Engineering.

 - Testing Experience: Experience designing and conducting test protocols.

 - Electrochemical Testing: Familiarity with cell stack testing - fuel cells, electrolysers, flow batteries, electrodialysers or similar.

 - Data Skills: Strong in data analysis and presenting results clearly.




ESSENTIAL TRAITS

 - Methodical & Detail-Oriented - You work carefully and focus on the details

 - Troubleshooter - You identify issues and maintain test stands

 - Bias for action - You prototype, test, and ship while others are still planning

 - Comfortable with intensity - 12-hour days and weekend sprints don't scare you, they energize you

 - Ego second - You care more about winning than being right

 - Figure things out independently - No hand-holding. You own problems end-to-end

 - Excellence as standard - Mistakes happen, but mediocrity doesn't




BONUS POINTS

 - Test Automation: Basic scripting knowledge (LabVIEW, Matlab, Python, R-Studio).

 - Electronics Qualification: Formal qualification in electronics for high-voltage DC/AC




HOW WE WORK

In-person matters: We work fully onsite in Ismaning, Germany. Our pilot plant is here, our breakthroughs happen here, and our team wins here—together.

Goals matter: We do everything to achieve our goals. If your workstream is the bottleneck, you don't stop. This isn't about serving hours, it's abo

source_jobs.title: Internship in Electrochemical Engineering (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
Shape the Future of Climate Tech with Phlair!

Fossil fuels have powered our economy for decades—at the cost of pollution and a changing climate. At Phlair, we’re reversing that equation. With our cost-efficient, scalable electrochemical Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology, we pull CO₂ from the air to either store it permanently or repurpose it into CO₂-negative chemicals. Join us in enabling a carbon-negative future.




WHY WE’RE DIFFERENT?

We’ve developed a breakthrough hydrolizer for acid/base regeneration, enabling scalable CO₂ capture. With partners like Frontier Climate, Stripe, and Shopify, we’re on track to make a global impact.

Our Project Dawn is our flagship initiative, setting the foundation to scale our technology and remove millions of tons of CO₂ from the atmosphere. We're one of the only seven DAC companies with a proven offtake agreement. Phlair is poised to become a technology leader, leveraging renewable energy and low-cost DAC to provide clean energy solutions that go beyond carbon removal.




IS THIS A FIT FOR ME?

We're building for the 3-5% who are energized by winning and thrive under pressure. Our pace demands people who see excellence as non-negotiable and execution as everything. If you need extensive structure or prefer playing it safe, we're probably not the right fit.




YOUR MISSION AT PHLAIR

You see a development stack and instinctively want to put it through its paces. Not just run the tests, but truly understand whether it works. You'll bring a methodical mindset and real attention to detail, taking full responsibility for the stacks within your testing remit. You're comfortable writing basic scripts to automate test routines and are familiar with test automation tools.

 - Take end-to-end ownership of a stack testbench

 - Have a hands-on approach to testbench improvements and maintenance

 - Independently complete a suite of electrochemical characterization techniques and tests

 - Process, analyze and report electrochemical data

 - Refine electrochemical testing operations to ensure consistent, high-quality results.

 - Contribute to an organized and clean testing area




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Successfully completed a bachelor or master studies in Chemistry or Chemical Engineering

 - Electrochemistry or Electrochemical knowledge (water electrolysis, hydrogen fuel cells, Power2X, or redox flow batteries)

 - You have experience and thrive in a lab environment

 - Ability to work with us for at least 6 months




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Methodical & Detail-Oriented — You work carefully and focus on the details

 - Reliable & Independent — You work autonomously, taking ownership of tasks ensuring completion on time.

 - Team over ego — You care more about collective wins than being right

 - Thrive in fast-paced environments — You're energized by ambitious timelines and meaningful challenges.




GREAT TO HAVE, BUT OPTIONAL

 - Previous experience in electrochemical technologies

 - Test bench experience: You’ve built and maintained electrochemical test benches

 - Hands on experience using electrochemical techniques




HOW WE WORK AT PHLAIR

 - Onsite & Collaborative: Our offices in Ismaning, Germany, house our pilot plant, where we work physically on our product every day. The team collaborates closely in the office, five days a week.




WHAT WE OFFER?

 - Competitive salary and stock options to share in the company’s success

 - 20 vacation days

 - Public transport pass for all of Germany

 - Fitness and wellness benefits to keep you at your best

 - Fresh team lunches, prepared by our amazing chef, Amelie

 - Direct leadership access to our CEO, CTO, and CSO

source_jobs.title: Working Student / Intern – Control Team (Systems & Data Infrastructure) (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
🌍 Shape the Future of Climate Tech with Phlair!

Fossil fuels have powered our economy for decades—at the cost of pollution and a changing climate. At Phlair, we’re reversing that equation. With our cost-efficient, scalable electrochemical Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology, we pull CO₂ from the air to either store it permanently or repurpose it into CO₂-negative chemicals. Join us in enabling a carbon-negative future.




🚀 WHY WE’RE DIFFERENT?

We’ve developed a breakthrough hydrolizer for acid/base regeneration, enabling scalable CO₂ capture. With partners like Frontier Climate, Stripe, and Shopify, we’re on track to make a global impact.

Our Project Dawn is our flagship initiative, setting the foundation to scale our technology and remove millions of tons of CO₂ from the atmosphere. We're one of the only seven DAC companies with a proven offtake agreement. Phlair is poised to become a technology leader, leveraging renewable energy and low-cost DAC to provide clean energy solutions that go beyond carbon removal.




🛠️ YOUR MISSION AT PHLAIR

 - IoT & Data Infrastructure: Build and manage the pipeline capturing data from our Beckhoff PLCs via OPC-UA and other fieldbus technologies.

 - Database Mastery: Structure and maintain the databases (e.g., InfluxDB, PostgreSQL) that store our experimental data, ensuring it is clean and scalable.

 - Visualization & "Deep Insight" Tools: Own our Grafana stack. Create intuitive dashboards that allow our scientists to compare different experiments in a highly visual and seamless fashion.

 - Full-Stack Automation: Develop internal tools and AI-enabled workflows (using Python or Claude Code) to automate the transition from raw experiment to reported insight.

System Reliability: Take responsibility for the health of our data flow from the sensor level to the cloud—no data gaps, no downtime.




🥼 WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

 - Education: Currently enrolled or completed studies in Computer Science, Mechatronics, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field.

 - The "Control" Toolkit:
   
   - Proven experience with Databases and Grafana.
   
   - Understanding of IoT architectures and industrial protocols (OPC-UA / Beckhoff PLC experience is a massive plus).
   
   - Proficient in Python for data processing and automation.

 - High Ownership: You thrive in unstructured environments. You don't wait for a manual; you write it.

 - Attention to Detail: You understand that in hardware-software integration, the smallest configuration error can invalidate weeks of testing.

 - Commitment: Available on-site for at least 6 months.

 - Adaptability: You will get pulled in to side quests often when hands are needed, especially in mechanical build out

 - Bridge the Gap: You possess the ability to translate complex scientific requirements into robust technical specifications. You listen to the needs of the lab and deliver solutions that solve their specific pain points.




🏭 HOW WE WORK AT PHLAIR

 - Onsite & Collaborative: Our offices in Ismaning, Germany, house our pilot plant, where we work physically on our product every day. The team collaborates closely in the office, five days a week.

 - Main Hub & Expansion: Our primary location is in Munich (Ismaning), Germany, and we’re launching our Edmonton, Canada team in late 2025/2026.




🌱 WHAT WE OFFER?

 - Competitive salary and stock options to share in the company’s success

 - 30 vacation days, including 2 days of recreational leave

 - Public transport pass for all of Germany

 - Relocation support to help you settle into Munich

 - Fitness and wellness benefits to keep you at your best

 - Fresh team lunches, prepared by our amazing chef, Amelie

 - Direct leadership access to our CEO, CTO, and CSO




🔗 LEARN MORE ABOUT PHLAIR

 - Our Mission, Vision and Team https://phlair.com/careers

 - Phlair Secures Frontier’s First Electrochemical DAC Deal https://frontierclimate.com/writing/phlair

 - Technology Behind Phlair https://techc

source_jobs.title: Chemielaborant (w/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
🌍 Gestalte die Zukunft der Klimatechnologie mit Phlair!

Fossile Brennstoffe haben unsere Wirtschaft jahrzehntelang angetrieben – auf Kosten von Umweltverschmutzung und einem sich wandelnden Klima. Bei Phlair drehen wir diese Gleichung um. Mit unserer kosteneffizienten, skalierbaren elektrochemischen Direct-Air-Capture-(DAC-)Technologie entziehen wir der Luft CO₂, um es entweder dauerhaft zu speichern oder in CO₂-negative Chemikalien umzuwandeln. Werde Teil unserer Mission für eine kohlenstoffneutrale Zukunft.




🚀 WAS UNS VON ANDEREN UNTERSCHEIDET?

Wir haben einen bahnbrechenden Hydrolyseur zur Säure-/Basen-Regeneration entwickelt, der eine skalierbare CO₂-Abscheidung ermöglicht. Mit Partnern wie Frontier Climate, Stripe und Shopify sind wir auf dem besten Weg, global etwas zu bewegen.

Unser Project Dawn ist unsere Leuchtturm-Initiative und legt das Fundament, um unsere Technologie zu skalieren und Millionen Tonnen CO₂ aus der Atmosphäre zu entfernen. Wir sind eines von nur sieben DAC-Unternehmen mit einer nachgewiesenen Abnahmevereinbarung. Phlair ist auf dem besten Weg, zum Technologieführer zu werden – mit erneuerbarer Energie und kostengünstigem DAC für saubere Energielösungen, die über die reine CO₂-Entfernung hinausgehen.




🛠️ DEINE MISSION BEI PHLAIR

 - Routinearbeit in unserem chemischen Labor

 - Du arbeitest auf der chemischen Analytik oder der Durchführung von standardisierten Experimenten

 - Labororganisation: Einkauf von Verbrauchsmaterial, Kalibrierung von Waagen und Pipetten, Herstellen von Standardlösungen




🥼 DAS BRINGST DU MIT

 - Erfahrung mit Techniken, die gerade im Einsatz sind (Titration, CSB-Bestimmung, ICP-OES).

 - Erfahrung und Motivation, Methoden eigenständig zu verbessern oder zu entwickeln.

 - Ausbildung zum Chemielaborant

 - Laborerfahrung (abgedeckt über die Ausbildung)

 - Erfahrung in analytischer Chemie, insbesondere in nasschemischen Verfahren

 - Selbstorganisation, Präzision und Genauigkeit!

 - Gutes Auge für Ordnung im Labor.

 - Ein Gefühl dafür, was genau wann gebraucht wird.

 - Soziale Fähigkeiten: gute Zusammenarbeit mit Kollegen, Kommunikation, was möglich ist und was nicht (beeinhaltet auch, weniger erfahrene Kollegen darauf aufmerksam zu machen, wie Laborequipment richtig zu verwenden ist).




💡OPTIONAL

 - Interesse daran, kleinere Forschungsprojekte oder Methodenentwicklungen selbst zu übernehmen.




🏭 SO ARBEITEN WIR BEI PHLAIR

 - Vor Ort & gemeinsam: In unseren Büros in Ismaning, Deutschland, befindet sich unsere Pilotanlage, an der wir täglich physisch an unserem Produkt arbeiten. Das Team arbeitet fünf Tage die Woche eng im Büro zusammen.




🌱 WHAT WE OFFER?

 - Wettbewerbsfähiges Gehalt und Aktienoptionen, um am Erfolg des Unternehmens teilzuhaben

 - 30 Urlaubstage, inklusive 2 Tagen Erholungsurlaub

 - Deutschlandweites ÖPNV-Ticket

 - Umzugsunterstützung, damit du gut in München ankommst

 - Fitness- und Wellness-Angebote, damit du in Bestform bleibst

 - Frische Team-Lunches, zubereitet von unserer großartigen Köchin Amelie

 - Direkter Draht zu unserem CEO, CTO und CSO




🔗 LEARN MORE ABOUT PHLAIR

 - Our Mission, Vision and Team https://phlair.com/careers

 - Phlair Secures Frontier’s First Electrochemical DAC Deal https://frontierclimate.com/writing/phlair

 - Technology Behind Phlair https://techcrunch.com/2024/09/19/phlairs-carbon-sucking-technology-could-lower-direct-air-captures-costs/

 - The Future of DAC https://medium.com/extantia-capital/will-direct-air-capture-ever-be-affordable-the-rise-of-dac-3-0-d46cb355f35c

------

Not ticking every box? No problem. We value potential and are open to considering a combination of education and experience instead of strictly following the listed requirements. If you believe you're a good fit for the role, even if your background differs slightly, please feel free to explain and highlight your key spikes in your career in your application.

We are committed to build a high-performing team where people f

source_jobs.title: Technical Procurement (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
🌎 WE'RE SOLVING HUMANITY'S BIGGEST CHALLENGE, AND IT TAKES EVERYTHING WE'VE GOT.

The world needs more energy, not less. Fossil fuels powered our economy for decades, at the cost of our planet. At Phlair, we're inverting that equation with breakthrough electrochemical Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology that pulls CO₂ from the air for permanent storage or repurposing into carbon-negative chemicals.

We're not here to play it safe. We're here to win.




WHY WE’RE DIFFERENT?

We solve hard problems and are product-first. Our hydrolyzer-based Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology, enabling scalable and low-cost CO2 capture from ambient air. The electrochemical technology allows off-the-shelf components and is ideally suited to the intermittent nature of renewable energy sources.

With proven offtake agreements from customers such as Google, McKinsey, JPMorganChase, Shopify, Stripe, H&M, and Deep Sky; we're one of only seven DAC companies with real market validation.

Project Dawn isn't just our flagship; it's our shot to scale CO₂ removal globally.




IS THIS A FIT FOR ME?

We're building for the 3-5% who are energized by winning and thrive under pressure. Our pace demands people who see excellence as non-negotiable and execution as everything. We cherish work-life-harmony, not balance. If you need extensive structure or prefer playing it safe, we're probably not the right fit.

Don't tick every box? We value potential and spikes over perfect resumes. If you believe you can contribute to our mission at this intensity level, tell us why.



Do you thrive on doing more with less?

We’re looking for someone who:

 - You love hunting for the best deal (if eBay Kleinanzeigen is in your top apps, you get us)

 - You like to dig deep on technical details in mechanical, process, or industrial engineering

 - You hate missing deadlines and no action

If this sounds like you, keep reading.




YOUR MISSION

Sourcing & Procurement

 - Source components and equipment packages for our upcoming plant: mechanical, chemical, and electrical

 - Set up and improve procurement systems that scale with the business

 - Ensure timely, cost-effective part acquisition in sync with technical needs and engineering schedule

Supplier Relationships

 - Build and manage strategic supplier partnerships

 - Negotiate pricing, performance, and Joint Development Agreements (JDAs)

 - Maintain alignment on specs, delivery, and quality

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with engineering to align specs, lead times, and sourcing options

 - Partner with finance and leadership to refine cost models and manage spend

Technical Expertise

 - Understand how complex systems fit together: materials, processes, tolerances, and trade-offs

 - Read technical drawings and challenge assumptions when needed

 - Confidently engage with engineers and suppliers on technical detail

Contract & Risk Management

 - Review and manage supplier contracts and agreements

 - Monitor market trends and supply chain risks, respond quickly and effectively

Continuous Improvement

 - Push improvements across sourcing, logistics, and procurement operations

 - Identify and implement unique ways to optimize cost, speed, and reliability

Your targets are your North Star. Nothing is set in stone in our world, we adapt fast, but we always hit our goals.




WHAT YOU BRING

Non-negotiable:

 - M.Sc. in Chemical, Mechanical, Industrial, or Process Engineering or related fields

 - Proven experience sourcing technical parts, running structured bidding processes and managing procurement in a hardware-heavy startup or scale-up

 - Strong understanding of cost drivers, accounting, and basic logistics

 - Sharp negotiation skills and a track record of building supplier relationships

 - Fluent in German and English

 - You’re organized, proactive, and cool under pressure

Essential Traits:

 - Bias for action - You prototype, test, and ship while others are still planning

 - Comfortable wit

notes: Phlair is a climate tech company with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
